title: Autonomous PR Feedback Remediation Loop
goal: Build a fully autonomous system that detects PR feedback (human + CodeRabbit), routes it to the original agent with context, has the agent critically evaluate each item (fix or deny with reason), auto-resolves threads, handles CI failures, and creates an auditable paper trail — all without manual intervention.

milestones.title: Foundation: Types, Events, Persistence
milestones.description: Extend type system to support per-thread tracking, new events, new feature statuses, and persistent PR tracking state

phases.name: add-pull-request-review-webhook-type
phases.title: Add pull_request_review webhook type
phases.description: Extend libs/types/src/webhook.ts to support pull_request_review event type with review actions (submitted, edited, dismissed)
acceptanceCriteria: GitHubWebhookEvent includes 'pull_request_review'
GitHubPullRequestReviewAction type defined
GitHubPullRequestReviewWebhookPayload interface created
Type exports correctly in GitHubWebhookPayload union

phases.name: add-per-thread-feedback-tracking-types
phases.title: Add per-thread feedback tracking types
phases.description: Extend libs/types/src/coderabbit.ts and feature.ts to track individual review threads with agent decisions (accept/deny) and reasoning
acceptanceCriteria: ReviewThreadFeedback interface with threadId, status (pending|accepted|denied), agentReasoning, resolvedAt
Feature type has threadFeedback array field
Feature type has remediationHistory array with timestamps and iteration metadata
Types compile and export correctly

phases.name: add-new-pr-remediation-events
phases.title: Add new PR remediation events
phases.description: Extend libs/types/src/event.ts with pr:remediation-started, pr:thread-evaluated, pr:threads-resolved events
acceptanceCriteria: AutomakerEventType includes new remediation events
Event payload types defined for each new event
Events documented with when/why they fire
Types compile and export correctly

phases.name: add-remediating-feature-status
phases.title: Add 'remediating' feature status
phases.description: Extend Feature status type to include 'remediating' state and update status transition logic
acceptanceCriteria: Feature status union includes 'remediating'
Status transition: review → remediating → review (on fixes) or done (on approval)
Feature loader handles new status correctly
UI can render remediating status (if feature-loader is only backend change, mark this N/A)

milestones.title: Webhook & Detection
milestones.description: Add instant PR review detection via webhooks with polling fallback

phases.name: implement-pull-request-review-webhook-handler
phases.title: Implement pull_request_review webhook handler
phases.description: Add handler in apps/server/src/routes/github/routes/webhook.ts that emits pr:review-submitted event
acceptanceCriteria: Handler processes pull_request_review webhook events
Emits pr:review-submitted with review data and PR number
Extracts review decision (approved, changes_requested, commented)
Signature verification works correctly
Logs webhook receipt and processing

phases.name: subscribe-prfeedbackservice-to-webhook-events
phases.title: Subscribe PRFeedbackService to webhook events
phases.description: Make PRFeedbackService listen to pr:review-submitted and immediately process reviews instead of waiting for poll
acceptanceCriteria: Service subscribes to pr:review-submitted event on init
On event, immediately calls processReviewStatus with PR data
Falls back to polling if webhook missed or delayed
Deduplicates webhook + poll (don't double-process same review)
Logs whether detection was webhook or poll

milestones.title: Persistence & State Recovery
milestones.description: Persist PR tracking state to disk and restore on server restart

phases.name: persist-tracked-prs-to-feature-json
phases.title: Persist tracked PRs to feature.json
phases.description: Store trackedPRs state in feature.json fields (prTrackedSince, prLastPolledAt) and restore on service init
acceptanceCriteria: Feature type has prTrackedSince and prLastPolledAt timestamp fields
On PR creation, save tracking metadata to feature.json
On service init, restore tracking for features with status=review and prNumber set
Restored PRs resume polling from last state
Test: server restart doesn't lose PR tracking

milestones.title: Feedback Triage & Agent Context
milestones.description: Parse feedback into per-thread items, load agent's previous context, and prepare structured remediation prompt

phases.name: build-structured-feedback-triage
phases.title: Build structured feedback triage
phases.description: Create buildThreadFeedbackPrompt that parses review into per-thread items with threadId, severity, message, location, and suggested fix
acceptanceCriteria: Fetches review threads via GraphQL reviewThreads query
Maps threads to structured feedback items with ID, severity, category, location
Groups CodeRabbit threads separately from human threads
Returns markdown prompt with numbered feedback items
Each item has clear Accept/Deny decision instruction

phases.name: load-agent-previous-context-on-restart
phases.title: Load agent previous context on restart
phases.description: Modify continuation prompt builder to include previous agent-output.md content so agent sees its own work
acceptanceCriteria: Reads .automaker/features/{id}/agent-output.md
Truncates if > 50k chars (keep last 40k to preserve recent context)
Prepends previous context to feedback prompt with clear separator
Prompt structure: previous work → separator → per-thread feedback → instructions
Handles missing agent-output.md gracefully (first iteration)

milestones.title: Antagonistic Review & Thread Resolution
milestones.description: Agent evaluates each feedback item critically, implements fixes, and auto-resolves/denies threads with reasoning

phases.name: add-thread-evaluation-instructions-to-prompt
phases.title: Add thread evaluation instructions to prompt
phases.description: Extend feedback prompt to require agent to evaluate each thread critically and output decision in structured format
acceptanceCriteria: Prompt includes evaluation criteria (does it improve code? is it aligned with project goals? is it feasible?)
Instructions require agent to output decisions in markdown table: ThreadID | Decision | Reasoning
Agent must justify BOTH accepts and denials
Clear examples of good vs bad feedback included in prompt
Agent can mark threads as 'needs-clarification' with questions

phases.name: parse-agent-thread-decisions-from-output
phases.title: Parse agent thread decisions from output
phases.description: After agent run, parse agent-output.md to extract per-thread decisions (accept/deny/clarify + reasoning)
acceptanceCriteria: Regex or markdown parser extracts decision table from agent output
Maps threadId → {decision, reasoning}
Validates all threads were addressed by agent
Stores decisions in feature.threadFeedback array
Logs unparseable or missing decisions as warnings

phases.name: auto-resolve-accepted-threads-after-push
phases.title: Auto-resolve accepted threads after push
phases.description: After agent pushes fixes, auto-call codeRabbitResolverService to resolve threads marked as 'accepted'
acceptanceCriteria: After agent completes and pushes, filter threadFeedback for decision=accepted
Call codeRabbitResolverService.resolveThreads with accepted threadIds
Update feature.threadFeedback status to 'resolved' with timestamp
Emit pr:threads-resolved event with count and threadIds
Handles GraphQL errors gracefully (log, don't fail entire flow)

phases.name: post-denial-comments-for-rejected-feedback
phases.title: Post denial comments for rejected feedback
phases.description: For threads marked 'denied', post a comment via GitHub API with agent's reasoning
acceptanceCriteria: For each denied thread, post reply comment via gh pr comment or GraphQL
Comment format: Agent evaluated this feedback and declined because: {reasoning}
Update feature.threadFeedback status to 'denied' with timestamp
Does NOT resolve the thread (leaves it open for human to see)
Emits pr:thread-evaluated event per denial

milestones.title: CI Failure Loop
milestones.description: Detect CI failures after agent pushes fixes and trigger another remediation cycle

phases.name: subscribe-to-pr-ci-failure-events
phases.title: Subscribe to pr:ci-failure events
phases.description: Make PRFeedbackService subscribe to pr:ci-failure and trigger another agent run with CI failure context
acceptanceCriteria: Service subscribes to pr:ci-failure event on init
On CI failure, increment prIterationCount
Build CI failure continuation prompt with failed check details
Restart agent with CI context (which checks failed, error messages)
Respects max iteration limit (escalate if > 2 CI failures)

phases.name: detect-ci-failures-after-push
phases.title: Detect CI failures after push
phases.description: After agent pushes, poll GitHub checks API to detect failures and emit pr:ci-failure event
acceptanceCriteria: After push detected (via webhook or poll), wait 30s then fetch check runs
If any required checks fail, emit pr:ci-failure with check names and conclusions
Extracts error messages from check output if available
Only triggers on REQUIRED checks (not optional)
Deduplicates (don't re-trigger on same failure)

milestones.title: Deconflict EM Agent & Cleanup
milestones.description: Remove duplicate pr:changes-requested handler from EM agent and clean up race conditions

phases.name: remove-em-pr-changes-requested-handler
phases.title: Remove EM pr:changes-requested handler
phases.description: Delete handleChangesRequested from em-agent.ts and remove subscription, let PRFeedbackService own feedback loop
acceptanceCriteria: handleChangesRequested method deleted
pr:changes-requested subscription removed from listenForPRFeedback
EM agent still handles pr:approved for auto-merge
No functionality broken (test merge-on-approval still works)
No race condition with PRFeedbackService

phases.name: stop-em-from-polluting-feature-description
phases.title: Stop EM from polluting feature.description
phases.description: Remove code that appends review feedback to feature.description permanently in EM agent
acceptanceCriteria: Lines 203-214 that append feedback to description are deleted
Description stays clean across PR iterations
Feedback stored only in feature.lastReviewFeedback and feature.threadFeedback
Original feature intent remains clear
Test: description doesn't grow after multiple feedback cycles

milestones.title: Integration & Testing
milestones.description: Wire everything together, add logging, handle edge cases, and validate end-to-end flow

phases.name: add-comprehensive-logging
phases.title: Add comprehensive logging
phases.description: Add structured logs at each step of remediation loop for debugging and audit trail
acceptanceCriteria: Log: feedback detected (webhook vs poll, review type)
Log: triage result (thread count, severity distribution)
Log: agent evaluation decisions (accepted/denied counts)
Log: thread resolution results (success/failure per thread)
Log: CI check results after push
All logs include featureId, prNumber, iteration for traceability

phases.name: handle-commented-reviews
phases.title: Handle COMMENTED reviews
phases.description: Analyze COMMENTED review decision to detect actionable CodeRabbit walk-throughs vs non-actionable comments
acceptanceCriteria: For COMMENTED reviews, parse comments to check for CodeRabbit suggestions
If CodeRabbit comment has severity markers, treat as actionable
If just walk-through summary with no suggestions, log and skip
Human COMMENTED reviews analyzed for keywords (should, must, needs) to detect actionability
Only trigger remediation if actionable items found

phases.name: end-to-end-flow-validation
phases.title: End-to-end flow validation
phases.description: Test complete remediation loop from feedback arrival to thread resolution to CI pass
acceptanceCriteria: Create test PR with CodeRabbit feedback → verify auto-remediation triggers
Verify agent sees previous context on restart
Verify accepted threads auto-resolve, denied threads get comment
Verify CI failure triggers another cycle
Verify state persists across server restart
Verify max iterations escalates correctly
All events fire in correct order

prd.situation: PRFeedbackService already polls every 60s and auto-restarts agents with feedback, CodeRabbitParserService parses structured comments, CodeRabbitResolverService can resolve threads (but only manually), and EMAgent handles approvals. However, 9 critical gaps prevent true autonomy: race conditions between services, no auto-thread-resolution, no CI failure loop, in-memory tracking lost on restart, agents lose context on restart, COMMENTED reviews ignored, no per-thread tracking, no critical evaluation of feedback (blind acceptance), and misleading feature status transitions.
prd.problem: When CodeRabbit or a human leaves PR feedback, the system can detect and restart the agent, but the loop isn't deterministic or complete. Agents blindly accept all feedback without evaluation. Review threads stay unresolved even after fixes. CI failures after fixes become dead ends. Tracking state is lost on server restart. The feature status cycles through 'backlog' misleadingly. There's no paper trail showing which feedback was accepted vs denied and why. Two services race to handle the same event causing state corruption. Agents don't see their previous work context when restarted for fixes.
prd.approach: Consolidate all feedback orchestration into PRFeedbackService (remove EM duplication). Add feedback triage step: parse each thread, classify actionable vs non-actionable, present to agent for critical evaluation. Agent gets previous context (agent-output.md) PLUS structured per-thread feedback. After agent pushes fixes: auto-run thread resolution to close addressed items, post denial reasons for rejected feedback. Subscribe to pr:ci-failure events and trigger remediation cycle. Persist tracked PRs to disk (extend feature.json). Add pull_request_review webhook for instant detection (keep polling as fallback). Introduce 'remediating' feature status to avoid misleading backlog transitions. Add per-thread tracking to feature metadata. Build antagonistic review capability: agent must justify accepting or denying each feedback item.
prd.results: When feedback arrives, the system will: (1) Instantly detect via webhook or 60s polling fallback. (2) Parse into structured per-thread items with severity/location. (3) Load agent's previous context (agent-output.md, executionHistory). (4) Present feedback to agent for critical evaluation. (5) Agent decides per-thread: accept+fix OR deny+reason. (6) Agent implements accepted fixes. (7) Auto-resolve addressed threads, post denial comments with reasoning. (8) Verify CI passes; if fails, trigger another remediation cycle. (9) All decisions logged to feature.json with timestamps and reasoning. (10) No human intervention needed; full audit trail exists. State survives server restarts. No race conditions or corruption.
constraints: Must maintain backward compatibility with existing auto-mode flow
Cannot break current EM agent merge workflow for approvals
Must work with existing feature.json schema (extend, don't replace)
CodeRabbit GraphQL API rate limits (max 100 threads per query)
GitHub webhook signature verification required for security
Agent context from previous run may be very large (handle truncation)
Some feedback may be subjective (agent needs clear evaluation criteria)
PR iteration limit (currently 2) must remain configurable
